name: geo-database
description: Access NCBI GEO for gene expression/genomics data. Search/download microarray and RNA-seq datasets (GSE, GSM, GPL), retrieve SOFT/Matrix files, for transcriptomics and expression analysis.
capabilities: Search GEO DataSets and Profiles using NCBI E-utilities API
Download series matrix files, SOFT files, and supplementary data via FTP
Parse GEO data using GEOparse library with code examples
Extract expression matrices and sample metadata from GSE records
Perform differential expression analysis on downloaded datasets
Batch process multiple GEO series for meta-analysis
author: davila7
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation and code examples for accessing NCBI's Gene Expression Omnibus database. All 258 static findings are false positives caused by pattern matching against markdown code blocks (triple backticks), bioinformatics terminology (SAM samples, ch2 channels), legitimate NCBI API URLs, and GEO accession number patterns.
valueStatement: Finding and processing gene expression datasets requires navigating complex database structures and file formats. This skill provides ready-to-use code patterns for searching, downloading, and analyzing GEO microarray and RNA-seq data.
userTitle: Search and download gene expression data from NCBI GEO
limitations: Requires external Python libraries (GEOparse, Biopython) to be installed
Does not provide direct database querying - teaches API usage patterns
Large datasets may require significant disk space and memory
NCBI rate limits apply (3 requests/second without API key)

useCases.title: Download cancer gene expression datasets
useCases.description: Search for breast cancer RNA-seq studies, download expression matrices, and prepare data for differential expression analysis.

useCases.title: Access published study data
useCases.description: Retrieve supplementary data from a paper's GEO accession number to reproduce or extend published analyses.

useCases.title: Build gene expression meta-analysis pipeline
useCases.description: Batch download multiple related studies and combine expression data across platforms for meta-analysis.

promptTemplates.title: Basic GEO search
promptTemplates.scenario: Find datasets for a specific disease
promptTemplates.prompt: Search GEO for human breast cancer RNA-seq datasets from the last 2 years and show me how to retrieve their metadata.

promptTemplates.title: Download expression data
promptTemplates.scenario: Get data from a known accession
promptTemplates.prompt: Download the expression matrix from GSE123456 using GEOparse and show me the sample information.

promptTemplates.title: Platform annotation mapping
promptTemplates.prompt: I have expression data from GPL570 platform. Help me map the probe IDs to gene symbols.

promptTemplates.title: Differential expression workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: I downloaded GSE123456 with treatment and control samples. Set up a differential expression analysis workflow.

bestPractices: Always set Entrez.email before using E-utilities to comply with NCBI policies
Cache downloaded GEO files locally to avoid repeated downloads and respect rate limits
Verify platform annotations are current as gene symbols may have been updated since submission
antiPatterns: Making rapid repeated requests without delays - this violates NCBI rate limits and may get you blocked
Assuming all expression values are comparable - check if data is raw, normalized, or log-transformed
Ignoring batch effects when combining data from multiple studies or platforms

faq.question: What is the difference between GSE, GSM, and GPL accessions?
faq.answer: GSE is a series (complete experiment), GSM is a sample (single data point), and GPL is a platform (array/sequencing technology definition).

faq.question: How do I increase the NCBI request rate limit?
faq.answer: Register for a free NCBI API key at ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/account to increase from 3 to 10 requests per second.

faq.question: What Python library should I use to access GEO?
faq.answer: GEOparse is recommended for downloading and parsing GEO data. Biopython provides E-utilities access for metadata searches.

faq.question: How do I handle large GEO datasets that cause memory errors?
faq.answer: Process data in chunks, use float32 instead of float64 for expression values, or download only the samples you need.

faq.question: Can I download raw sequencing data from GEO?
faq.answer: Raw sequencing files are stored in SRA. GEO links to SRA accessions. Use SRA toolkit or GEOparse with download_sra=True.

faq.question: What file format should I download for expression analysis?
faq.answer: Series matrix files are fastest for getting expression data. SOFT files include complete metadata. Both are supported by GEOparse.
